projects.title: Unit 3 Project — Ratios in Action
projects.desc: Capstone project: apply ratio reasoning to a real-world challenge.

projects.title: Ratio Rally
projects.desc: Solve ratio and rate problems in a fast-paced game.

projects.title: Unit Rate Duel
projects.desc: Solve real-world rate problems by finding unit rates.

projects.title: Unit 3 Graphic Novel

title: Ratio and Rate Problem Solving
contentObjective: I can solve real-world rate problems by finding and using unit rates.
languageObjective: I can explain my solution using the words rate, unit rate, per, and proportion.

turnAndTalk.question: Team 1 knows 3 pounds of chicken cost $18. What was your plan to find the cost of just 1 pound, and why is that unit rate useful?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Divide the total cost by the number of pounds to find the cost per 1 pound.

stems.en: My plan was to divide ___ by ___ to get the cost per ___.
stems.es: Mi plan fue dividir ___ entre ___ para hallar el costo por ___.

stems.en: The unit rate is ___ per pound, which is useful because ___.
stems.es: La tasa unitaria es ___ por libra, lo cual es útil porque ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students divide $18 by 3 to get $6 per pound and explain the unit rate lets them find the cost of any number of pounds.
turnAndTalk.extend: Once you know the unit rate is $6 per pound, how can you find the cost of 7 pounds without a ratio table? Justify your shortcut.
extendStems: I can find the cost of 7 pounds by ___ because ___.
The unit rate works as a shortcut since ___.

turnAndTalk.question: Team 2 frosts 5 cupcakes every 4 minutes. How did you set up a rate or proportion to find how many cupcakes they frost in a longer time?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Use the rate of 5 cupcakes per 4 minutes, or find cupcakes per 1 minute, then scale.

stems.en: The rate is ___ cupcakes per ___ minutes.
stems.es: La tasa es ___ cupcakes por ___ minutos.

stems.en: To find the total, I ___ the rate.
stems.es: Para hallar el total, ___ la tasa.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students state the rate (5 cupcakes per 4 minutes) and set up a proportion or unit rate (1.25 cupcakes/min) to scale to the needed time.
turnAndTalk.extend: Would it be faster to use a unit rate or a proportion for this problem? Explain when each strategy is the better tool.
extendStems: I would use a ___ here because ___.
A proportion is better when ___, but a unit rate is better when ___.

turnAndTalk.question: Tell about a real situation where rates matter, like speed, pay, or price, and explain how the unit rate helps you.
turnAndTalk.kernel: Rates like miles per hour or dollars per pound let us predict and compare amounts.

stems.es: Las tasas importan cuando ___, como ___ por ___.

stems.en: The unit rate helps me because ___.
stems.es: La tasa unitaria me ayuda porque ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students give a real rate (speed, hourly pay, price per item) and explain how the unit rate lets them predict or compare.
turnAndTalk.extend: Pick two real rates that could be compared, and explain how unit rates would let you decide which is the better choice.
extendStems: I could compare ___ and ___ by finding ___.
The better choice would be the one with ___ because ___.

vocabulary.definition: A ratio comparing two amounts with different units, like miles per hour.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una razón que compara dos cantidades con unidades distintas, como millas por hora.
vocabulary.cloze: A ratio comparing two quantities with different units is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: A rate for just 1 of something, like cost for 1 item.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una tasa para solo 1 de algo, como el precio de 1 artículo.
vocabulary.cloze: A rate for exactly one unit, like $3 per 1 pound, is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: For each one. Example: 5 dollars per book.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Por cada uno. Ejemplo: 5 dólares por libro.
vocabulary.cloze: The word that means 'for each' in a rate, like miles ___ hour, is ___.

vocabulary.definition: Using ratios and rates to find a missing amount.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Usar razones y tasas para encontrar una cantidad que falta.
vocabulary.cloze: Using a plan and math steps to find an answer to a real situation is ___.

vocabulary.definition: A math sentence saying two ratios are equal. It helps find a missing number.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una oración matemática que dice que dos razones son iguales. Ayuda a hallar un número que falta.
vocabulary.cloze: An equation that two ratios are equal is a ___.

launch.narrative: It's the final day of Chef Academy — the Culinary Competition! Teams must solve ratio and rate problems to earn ingredients for their dishes. Team 1 needs to figure out how much chicken costs if 3 pounds cost $18. Team 2 must calculate how many cupcakes they can frost if they decorate 5 cupcakes every 4 minutes. The clock is ticking!
noticePrompts: What two quantities with different units are being compared?
How could you find the cost of just 1 pound of chicken?
How could you find the number of cupcakes frosted in 1 minute?
wonderPrompts: Which strategy is fastest — unit rates, ratio tables, or proportions?
What if the rate is not a whole number — can you still solve the problem?
launch.contextImage: A culinary competition stage with two teams, a timer, ingredient stations, and scoreboard

conceptIntro.heading: How do unit rates solve rate problems?
conceptIntro.intro: A rate compares two amounts with different units, like dollars and pounds. A unit rate is the amount for just 1. You find it by dividing, and you can use it to find any amount.
conceptIntro.keyIdea: Find the unit rate by dividing, then multiply it to find the amount you need.

iDo.title: Watch me
lines: Team 1 knows that 3 pounds of chicken cost $18.
To find the unit rate, I divide the cost by the pounds: 18 divided by 3 = $6 per pound.
Now I can find any amount. For 5 pounds, I multiply: 6 x 5 = $30.
The unit rate $6 per pound is the shortcut for every amount.

weDo.title: Let's try together
lines: A car drives 100 miles in 2 hours. What is the unit rate in miles per hour?
Divide: 100 divided by 2 = 50 miles per hour.
How far in 4 hours? Multiply: 50 x 4 = 200 miles.

youDo.title: Now it's your turn
lines: Next you will complete the chicken cost table and find the unit rate.
Divide to find the cost of 1 pound, then use it for other amounts.

explore.instructions: Team 1 knows that 3 pounds of chicken cost $18. Complete the ratio table to find the cost for different amounts, then determine the unit rate.

optional.stem: In the kitchen, 3 pizzas feed exactly 12 people. At the same rate, how many pizzas are needed to feed 20 people?
optional.explanation: First find the unit rate: 12 people ÷ 3 pizzas = 4 people per pizza. Then 20 people ÷ 4 people per pizza = 5 pizzas.

optional.stem: A line cook chops 8 carrots in 2 minutes. Working at the same steady rate, how many carrots can the cook chop in 7 minutes?
optional.explanation: Unit rate: 8 carrots ÷ 2 minutes = 4 carrots per minute. In 7 minutes: 4 × 7 = 28 carrots.

optional.label: Match each kitchen rate to its correct unit rate. Decide what is being compared first.

pairs.term: 24 cups of soup poured into 6 bowls
60 ounces of sauce split into 4 jars

optional.stem: Level 2 Extension — Mixed review: A recipe uses 18 ounces of flour to make 3 loaves of bread. How many ounces of flour are needed to make 7 loaves at the same rate?
optional.explanation: Unit rate: 18 ounces ÷ 3 loaves = 6 ounces per loaf. For 7 loaves: 6 × 7 = 42 ounces. The equivalent ratio 18/3 = 42/7 confirms it.

optional.title: Level 2 Extension — Catch the Recipe Mistake

workedExample.label: Read the problem
workedExample.work: 9 cups of batter fill 12 muffin cups. How many cups of batter are needed for 16 muffin cups?

workedExample.label: Set up the unit rate
workedExample.work: 12 muffins ÷ 9 cups = about 1.33 muffins per cup

workedExample.label: Answer
workedExample.work: About 21.3 cups of batter are needed

optional.correctWork: The unit rate was set up in the wrong order. Divide cups by muffins: 9 cups ÷ 12 muffins = 0.75 cup per muffin. Then 0.75 × 16 = 12 cups of batter.
hints: Look at Step 2. The question asks for cups of batter, so the unit rate should tell you cups per muffin, not muffins per cup.
Divide 9 cups by 12 muffins to get 0.75 cup per muffin, then multiply by 16 muffins to get 12 cups.

approaching.stem: A bakery makes 24 cookies in 3 batches. What is the unit rate of cookies per batch?
approaching.explanation: Unit rate: 24 cookies ÷ 3 batches = 8 cookies per batch.

approaching.stem: Apples cost $5 for 4 pounds. What is the cost per pound?
A cyclist rides 36 miles in 3 hours. At the same rate, how far will she ride in 5 hours?
approaching.explanation: Unit rate: 36 ÷ 3 = 12 miles per hour. In 5 hours: 12 × 5 = 60 miles.

approaching.stem: A printer prints 45 pages in 9 minutes. How many pages does it print per minute?

approaching.instructions: Find the unit rate for each situation.

onLevel.instructions: Team 2 frosts 5 cupcakes every 4 minutes. Complete the ratio table to find how many cupcakes they can frost in different times.

onLevel.stem: A car travels 150 miles in 3 hours. A bus travels 200 miles in 5 hours. Which vehicle is faster?
onLevel.explanation: Car: 150÷3 = 50 mph. Bus: 200÷5 = 40 mph. The car travels faster at 50 mph vs. 40 mph.

onLevel.instructions: Sort each rate problem by the strategy that works BEST to solve it.

items.text: Find cost per pound when 5 lb costs $12
If 3 shirts cost $24, find cost of 9 shirts
A car goes 55 mph — how far in 4 hours?
Scale a recipe from 4 servings to 20 servings

onLevel.label: A runner goes 6 miles in 48 minutes. Place markers showing distance at 0, 16, 32, and 48 minutes.

extending.title: Find Mia's Mistake

workedExample.label: Read the problem
workedExample.work: A store sells 6 oranges for $4.50. What is the cost of 10 oranges?

hints: Look at Step 3. Mia found the correct unit rate of $0.75, but what did she do with it?
To find the total cost, you multiply the unit rate by the number of items — not add.

extending.stem: A factory makes 240 widgets in 8 hours. Another factory makes 200 widgets in 5 hours. Which factory is faster? If both factories work together for 10 hours, how many total widgets would they produce?
extending.sampleAnswer: Factory A: 240 ÷ 8 = 30 widgets/hour. Factory B: 200 ÷ 5 = 40 widgets/hour. Factory B is faster. Together: (30 + 40) × 10 = 70 × 10 = 700 widgets in 10 hours.

extending.instructions: Two delivery services charge different rates. Service A: $3 per mile + $5 flat fee. Service B: $4 per mile, no flat fee. Complete the table and determine when each is cheaper.

optionalActivity.name: Ratio Problem-Solving Case File
optionalActivity.intro: Chef detectives crack each kitchen ratio mystery one case at a time.

connect.scenario: A runner trains for a 5K race. During practice, she runs 3 miles in 27 minutes. She wants to know her pace (minutes per mile) and predict how long the full 5K (3.1 miles) will take at the same rate.
connect.prompt: This is like our rate problem-solving work because ___ and ___ are related by ___.

exitTicket.stem: A printer prints 30 pages in 5 minutes. At this rate, how many pages will it print in 12 minutes?
exitTicket.explanation: Unit rate: 30 ÷ 5 = 6 pages per minute. In 12 minutes: 6 × 12 = 72 pages.
